description:
You are a mission analyst at an Earth observation company. Operations needs to know how high-order the gravity model needs to be for acceptable prediction accuracy for a new LEO imaging satellite. Higher orders cost more compute time. Your task:

1. Read the initial state document at ~/Documents/missions/gravity_study_initial_state.txt
2. Create a GMAT script that propagates this satellite for exactly 7 days (ElapsedDays = 7) under 4 different Earth gravity truncation levels:
   - J2-only (Degree=2, Order=0)
   - 4x4 (Degree=4, Order=4)
   - 12x12 (Degree=12, Order=12)
   - 70x70 reference (Degree=70, Order=70)
3. You MUST isolate gravity effects: Do NOT include atmospheric drag, SRP, or third-body point masses (Sun/Luna) in any force model.
4. Propagate identical initial conditions for all 4 scenarios.
5. Compute the final RSS position difference (in km) of each lower-fidelity model relative to the 70x70 reference model at the end of the 7 days.
6. Write a sensitivity report to ~/GMAT_output/gravity_sensitivity_report.txt containing EXACTLY these lines:
   J2_divergence_km: <value>
   4x4_divergence_km: <value>
   12x12_divergence_km: <value>
   ordering: <state the ordering, e.g., J2 > 4x4 > 12x12>
   recommended_min_order: <your recommendation>
7. Save your GMAT script to ~/Documents/missions/gravity_study.script and run it.

Login: username=ga, password=password123
